source_documents: https://www.fatf-gafi.org (Terrorist Financing Risk Assessment Guidance, Jul 2019)
risk_signals: Small, structured transfers or collections routed toward conflict or high-risk regions
Non-profit or charity accounts whose flows diverge from their stated purpose
Any nexus to sanctioned individuals, entities, or listed organisations
analyst_reasoning: Terror financing often involves small amounts (so it evades value-based thresholds) and legitimate-seeming vehicles like NPOs. Detection therefore leans on destination, purpose, and sanctions nexus rather than transaction size - the opposite emphasis to most laundering typologies.
investigation_steps: Screen all parties against sanctions and terrorist-designation lists
Assess NPO/charity flows for diversion from stated charitable purpose
Analyse geographic routing toward conflict/high-risk regions despite small values
decision: File an STR/relevant report immediately, freeze where a sanctions match exists, and escalate to the FIU and authorities.
outcome: Because amounts are small, TF is missed by value thresholds; sanctions and purpose screening are the effective controls.
lessons_learned: Terror financing is about destination, purpose, and sanctions nexus, not transaction size
NPO/charity misuse is a recognised TF channel requiring purpose-level scrutiny
Sanctions screening is a non-negotiable control, however small the amount
